title: thymus serpyllum subsp. alpestris
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. alpestris, commonly known as Alpine Thyme, is a subspecies of the Thymus serpyllum plant, a low-growing, perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, making it a popular natural remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Alpine Thyme include its ability to boost the immune system, improve digestion, and reduce stress and anxiety, while also exhibiting antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections. Alpine Thyme can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, and is often used in herbal medicine to support respiratory health, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
